item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Knowing that Avatar The Last Airbender is going through a bit of a renaissance, I really wish one of them was an earthbender.

https://youtu.be/H0U7jHSfh7M

item.title: So this is allowed now?
item.content:encoded: Seriously? The John Cena meme is a fucking SCP now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I presume "trying to game 3331-1-23's abilities" also counts as a game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to the difficulty associated with removing a personality as well known as John Cena from public knowledge, it was deemed more efficient to replace him with one of the non-anomalous SCP-3331-1 instances.

I don't buy this. They managed with Owen Hart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did not think this would work at first, and while I agree with a previous poster that the interview should have ended earlier, it was overall great.

Can one instance literally become invisible, or did I miss it?

+1

Edit: I did miss it. SCP-3331-1-27 did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised nobody's pointed out this, but it seems like the one summoning all this Cenas is the soul/ghost/whatever of the kid that got visited by the real John Cena and that the reason as to why it creates them is because (in my headcanon) the kid is braging about how he met him towards other children that died in the same hospital, and more often than not the kid always exagerates how 'cool' John Cena is. As a way to keep make the other kids belive him, he recreates John Cena again and again, but each instance matches perfectly how the kid previously described him as.

I think I'm digging in too much into a John Cena SCP. So I'll shut up now and give you my upvote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cena wins by points. Dies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My little brother was a huge Cena fan and I personally really enjoyed the melodramatic flourishes in the dialogue. That's not to imply that a future rework is out of the question, of course. It might go over some folks' heads, but have you considered watching old Cena interviews and promos and incorporating more characteristic bits of dialogue? Maybe even a containment team well-versed in WWE-related anomolies.

Overall great skip. Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those things where every time I think about it, I giggle a little bit. When I first started reading, I thought this was going to be a late cash in on the long-dead John Cena meme. Instead I was treated to John Cena delivering a comic book villain style monologue. One of the funniest SCP's I've read in a while.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with the above comments saying this would be better as a -J. I understand that the interview was meant to be cheesy and cliche, but that doesn't change the fact that it is cheesy and cliche. Even ignoring the John Cena meme, this is still an object that summons anomalous pro-wrestlers, one of which is a generic, "guy thats egotistical because he literally can't be beat."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not for me. make it a j article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the heck?!? Why do I like this?!? HOW THE [DATA EXPUNGED] DID YOU MANAGE TO MAKE JOHN CENA AN SCP?!?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if instance 23 challenged 682 to a "Wrestling match to the death"? Would this terminate 682 for good?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few critiques:

6 A memetic effect that causes observers to perceive it as being approximately 70cm taller than themselves. Effect translates through video recordings; actual height presumed to match that of John Cena, but cannot be confirmed.

This should be rather easy to test, like having him walk through a hall of laser motion sensors at various heights, or use a doorway that is John Cena's height, and have a computer time how long it took him to walk through (as in, whether he needed to duck or not). Or, more simply, just use a scale.

23 When directly engaged in any form of game, contest, or challenge, Instance 23 will always win, regardless of any prior experience in the specific game or how many other participants there are.

This seems super exploitable and could be used as a Thaumiel-class. "I bet you $10000 that we will have at least one containment breach this year". Seems like an "infinite wishes" scenario.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would have an issue with the dialog but then I consider how this SCP is apparently at least partially based on John Cena's persona, and thus is being written by a hack in the writer's room. So dropping all subtlety is totally appropriate.

But the reveal makes me wonder what Nikki thinks of all this. Does she know? Is she anomalous too?

item.title: Would be better as a -J article honestly...
item.content:encoded: I just don't feel like it is anywhere near believable enough to be a serious SCP entry. I might excuse the ridiculousness if it was actually funny but it just… wasn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cena: Unless you'd wiped my memory of it, of course.

Man. Mark me down for 'would prefer if interview ended here'. It takes all the tension out of the reveal for a cliche villain monologue to just drop in and smugly spell out the story for the reader. It would have been much more satisfying without all that considering you had such good momentum leading up to it.

Went from 'oh shit' to 'god, just stop talking'. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even in an SCP article John Cena finds a way to make a Face-Heel turn.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think you are meant to

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just can't take this seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well to be fair, you pretty much got the point - the dialogue is meant to seem like a movie villain monologue, because that's how this version seems himself. The master manipulator who has been pulling one over on the world for however long. If you don't like that, fair enough, but at least its getting across what it's meant to be.

And, for the record, it's not meant to be Internet Meme Article, but I can see why people would think that and I decided I was ok with that right from the start.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is really weird and i love it

take my +1

item.title: I could not get past the interview.
item.content:encoded: I'm echoing Kalinin's sentiments here. The dialogue is too clunky to sell the interview and I downvoted pretty much as soon as I got to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's more of a refined delivery vehicle for the "Internet Meme: The Article" concept, so there's that. Can't say that I'm a fan of attempts to do that, but this version of the article is a better attempt at conveying the intended narrative.

The replacement for the interview has traded one set of clunky dialogue for another, however. Now, we've got an unnecessary interview in which John Cena monologues about his nefarious plans, complete with Movie Villain flourish. It shares the same problem as before, in that it's the story being told in the most direct possible manner, in a way that feels very forced by what the story requires, rather than what actual people would say.

I doubt there's a version of this that I'd end up supporting, but this is exhibiting a lot of the same weaknesses as the previous iteration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather underwhelming until the interview. Everything thereafter was pure gold and more than enough for a very strong +1 :D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now you turned my tentative upvote to a solid upvote. Nice work.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So after posting this originally, I decided I wasn't super happy with it as I'd lost a few elements that were in original versions, so I took it down and reworked it, so here's Version 2 (actually closer to version 1000).

Thanks to the following for checking out the draft - sorry if I missed anyone, a lot of people looked at this one!
Hippo
ElectronicTree
Crowborn
DrBleep
DarkStuff
NineVolt
MaliceAforethought
Cimmerian
Westrin
thebillith
Alivi

With special thanks to Hippo for two pretty big sessions of helping me hammer out ideas.

You can check out my other work here!

Image is taken from here, released under CC3
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Situs_inversus_chest_Nevit.jpg
